other_observers: None; It was first reported by Kranik on April 3 on the Colordao RBA.  After I saw the bird I called Sue Riffe and she and 4 other people (grouse trip) saw the bird on the same day that I saw it.
specific_location: Elliot State Wildlife Area. It was about 1 mile East of the North Tract. The pond is on the north side of the road and on the south side of the road there was a parking area that on the sign that said Elliot State Wildlife Area, Wetland Restoration Project.
description: A large whitish bird. It had a lot of dark markings on the bird that makes it look like it is going into its first summer plumage. It was white on the sides and breast of the bird and gray on the head, neck, and back. It was gray The bill is black and has no yellow on it. It also came to a point (or "V") on the forhead. The bill was also pretty straight, not curved. The lores go to the eyes. It had black legs. It was much larger than the Canada Geese. The back appreared to be rounded.Behaviors: It was swimming around on a small pond, seemed like it was hanging out with several Canada Geese. It didn't act like it was very scared of a train that ran right beside the pond.Call: Never heard it callPlumage: Juvenile into first summer
